videoTitle: ☁️ DevOps Hands-On | AWS Cloud Fundamentals – Day 5(DevOpsFarm Day 41)
videoDescription:
Welcome to Day 5  of AWS Cloud in the DevOps Full Course by DevOpsFarm! 🚀
This in-depth, hands-on tutorial is your complete guide to AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management), the crucial, global service used to secure your AWS cloud environment.
                  We start by clarifying the purpose of IAM, which defines who can access your account and what they can do. You will learn the difference between the four core IAM identities: Users, Roles, Policies, and User Groups.

In this video, we cover:
➤ IAM Fundamentals: Understand IAM's role in security and why it's a global service, unlike regional services like EC2 and Lambda.

➤ Users vs. Roles: See how Users grant access to human beings, while Roles allow one AWS service (like an EC2 instance or Lambda function) to communicate with another (like S3).

➤ Hands-on User Creation: We walk through creating an IAM user, providing them a unique login URL, and setting up initial passwords.

➤ Permissions Deep Dive: Learn to attach permissions, test the concept of Access Denied for unauthorized services, and differentiate between Full Access and Read-Only Access.

➤ Granular Control with Policies: We explore AWS Managed Policies and show you how to create your own Customer Managed Policies to set ultra-low-level, granular permissions, such as limiting a user's access to only one specific S3 bucket.

➤ User Groups for Scale: Discover how using User Groups (Developer, QA, Manager) saves time and simplifies managing permissions for large teams.

➤ Third-Party Integration: Learn about Identity Providers and how they allow external systems like GitHub Actions, GitLab, or CircleCI to securely interact with your AWS account.

⏱️ Chapters:
00:00:18 
Introduction to AWS IAM (Identity and Access Management) 

00:00:50 
IAM is a Global Service (vs. Regional Services like EC2/Lambda) 

00:01:04 
The Four IAM Identities: Users, Roles, Policies, & Groups 

00:01:34 
IAM User vs. IAM Role (Human vs. Service Access) 

00:02:37 
IAM Policies (JSON-based Permissions & Attachment) 


00:03:14 
IAM User Groups (Simplifying Permission Management) 


00:04:26

videoTitle: Kubernetes Troubleshooting in Production: Real DevOps Debugging with Services, DNS & Netshoot
videoDescription:
In this hands-on Kubernetes troubleshooting session, we debug a realistic production-style deployment where everything looks healthy — Pods are running, Services are created, Deployments are green — yet the application does not work.

This lecture demonstrates real-world DevOps debugging techniques including:

Identifying Kubernetes Service port mismatches

Verifying traffic flow using Test-NetConnection / Telnet

Debugging from inside a Pod using kubectl exec

Understanding minimal container images

Using Netshoot for advanced network troubleshooting

Fixing cross-namespace DNS resolution issues

Testing database connectivity with Netcat (nc)

Differentiating ICMP vs TCP port checks

If you support Kubernetes in production, these troubleshooting skills are essential.

Perfect for: DevOps Engineers, SREs, Platform Engineers, Cloud Engineers, and Kubernetes administrators.

channelDescription:
Most infrastructure advice works in controlled demos. It often fails under real constraints.

SystemAxis explores how AI, cloud, and DevOps systems behave in production — under load, under pressure, and under real cost limits.

This channel focuses on practical engineering: stress testing configurations, analyzing bottlenecks, understanding failure modes, and making deliberate architectural decisions. Sometimes that means running controlled experiments. Sometimes it means breaking assumptions. Sometimes it means designing systems properly from the start.

Topics include AI infrastructure, cloud architecture, Linux internals, automation, cost control, platform engineering, and performance tuning.

The goal is simple: understand how systems actually behave - not how they are supposed to behave.

No hype. No demo-only advice. Just production reality.

videoTitle: ☁️ DevOps Hands-On | AWS Cloud Fundamentals – Day 4(DevOpsFarm Day 40)
videoDescription:
Welcome to Day 4 of AWS Cloud in the DevOps Full Course by DevOpsFarm! 🚀
This comprehensive, hands-on tutorial dives deep into AWS Serverless Lambda, a core service for building modern, cost-effective cloud applications. We start by contrasting Lambda with traditional EC2 instances and then guide you through the entire process of creating, deploying, testing, and monitoring your first Python Lambda function.

What you will learn in this video:
➤ Serverless Fundamentals: Understand why Lambda is the go-to choice for running code without managing servers (CPU, RAM, storage).

➤ Function Creation: Choose between creating a function from scratch, using a blueprint, or deploying a container image.

➤IAM Execution Roles: Configure the necessary Identity and Access Management (IAM) role to grant your Lambda function permissions to communicate with other AWS services like S3 or a database.

➤Deployment & Testing: Learn how to write code directly in the console, deploy changes, and use the built-in test feature with JSON event input.

➤Debugging and Logging: Use AWS CloudWatch Logs to find syntax errors, track invocations, and debug your function's execution details.

➤Triggers and Automation: See practical examples of setting up automated triggers, including event-based triggers (like file uploads to an S3 bucket) and scheduled Cron jobs using EventBridge.

➤Advanced Configurations: Cover crucial configurations such as setting up a public Lambda Function URL, using environment variables, adding tags for cost tracking, configuring a VPC for database communication, and using function versioning.

Bonus Topic: A brief introduction to interacting with AWS services using the AWS Command Line Interface (CLI).


Services Covered: AWS Lambda, IAM, EventBridge, CloudWatch, S3, EC2, RDS (Mentioned)

videoTitle: DevOps Hands-On | Ansible – Day 4  ( DevOpsFarm Day 46 )
videoDescription:
In this in-depth session, we dive into the critical world of DevOps monitoring and master the industry-leading open-source tools: Grafana, Prometheus, and Loki.

Learn why monitoring is essential for getting system feedback, taking proactive action, and preventing system failure due to issues like high CPU usage, low RAM, or disk space depletion. We cover the key aspects you must monitor, including system resources, logs, and traces.




What you will learn in this session:


The Importance of Monitoring: Understand the benefits, including anomaly detection, generating alerts, and creating useful dashboards for historical data analysis.



Core Monitoring Tools: An overview of popular tools like Grafana, New Relic, Datadog, and Dynatrace.



Prometheus Explained: A deep dive into Prometheus, the time-series database used for collecting and tracking metrics (like CPU usage over time).



Loki for Logging: Learn how Loki is used for log collection and parsing, making logs consumable by Grafana.



The Grafana Stack in Action: See how Grafana serves as the visualization layer, consuming data from Prometheus and Loki to create powerful dashboards and configure alerts.



Practical Demo: We demonstrate connecting data sources, exploring metrics using PromQL, and setting up label-based notification policies to ensure the right teams are alerted instantly.


Whether you are a beginner or a seasoned DevOps professional, this guide provides the knowledge and practical steps needed to implement robust monitoring for any application or microservice environment.

Pods were healthy.

This is operator knowledge — the kind interviewers look for but never explain.

videoTitle: Why Google & Netflix Love Prometheus | DevOps Explained
videoDescription:
Why do some of the biggest tech companies like Google and Netflix rely on Prometheus? In this short video, we break down the key reasons why Prometheus has become a cornerstone of modern DevOps monitoring.

Prometheus is a widely-used, open-source monitoring tool that is built to scale in complex cloud environments. It is fully cloud-native, meaning it integrates seamlessly with modern cloud infrastructure, including Kubernetes. Its open-source nature allows organizations to customize and extend it to meet their unique monitoring needs, while its scalability ensures it can handle massive workloads without slowing down.

These features make Prometheus an ideal choice for DevOps teams, Site Reliability Engineers (SREs), and cloud architects. Companies like Google and Netflix use it to monitor system metrics, detect issues before users notice, and ensure high uptime and reliability across their global infrastructure.

Understanding why Prometheus is so popular is essential for anyone building a career in DevOps or managing cloud-based systems. By adopting Prometheus, teams can proactively monitor their systems, improve performance, and maintain a seamless experience for users.

💡 Comment “PROMETHEUS” if you agree and want more DevOps tips. Follow this channel for quick, bite-sized DevOps tutorials.

videoTitle: The Phoenix Project Explained: How IT, DevOps & The Three Ways Transform Business Chaos into Flow
videoDescription:
Dive into the gripping narrative of "The Phoenix Project," a novel that unpacks the critical challenges faced by IT Operations and their profound impact on business success. Join us as we follow Bill Palmer's journey from a seasoned IT manager unexpectedly thrust into the VP of IT Operations role at Parts Unlimited, a company spiraling towards disaster.

This video explores how Bill, guided by the enigmatic board member Erik Reid, navigates a world of chronic outages, missed deadlines, and endless firefighting. We'll break down the revolutionary concepts introduced in the book, including:
   The Four Types of Work: Understanding business projects, internal IT projects, changes, and the "anti-work" of unplanned, recovery efforts.
   The Theory of Constraints (TOC): Learning to identify, exploit, subordinate, and elevate the system's bottleneck, exemplified by the overburdened engineer, Brent.
   The Three Ways of DevOps: Unpacking the principles of Flow, Feedback, and Continual Learning & Experimentation that drive IT transformation.
   Practical Implementations: Witnessing the "project freeze," the transformation of change management using Kanban, the strategic pivot of Project Unicorn, and how cross-functional collaboration ultimately saves Parts Unlimited from outsourcing and financial ruin.

Discover how these principles can help any organization move from IT chaos to predictable, efficient, and value-driven operations, ensuring IT becomes a strategic partner that truly helps the business win.

#ThePhoenixProject #DevOps  #LeanIT #ITManagement #DigitalTransformation #BusinessLeadership #ITIL #BookSummary #EnterpriseIT  #ContinuousDelivery #UnplannedWork #PhoenixProject

videoTitle: From Code to Cloud: Building Resilience Across the DevOps Pipeline | SHIFT 2025
videoDescription:
In this Shift session, Commvault and Oikocredit break down the growing risks inside modern DevOps environments and how enterprises can build true end-to-end resilience across cloud platforms. Featuring real-world insights from Oikocredit’s cloud engineering team and a full walkthrough of Commvault’s DevOps protection capabilities, this session reveals how to safeguard pipelines, repositories, metadata, and business-critical IP across GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, and more.

What This Session Covers
* The growing risks within DevOps environments—accidental deletions, insider actions, ransomware, configuration errors, cloud outages, and API limitations.
* Why DevOps is now strategic infrastructure holding your organization’s IP, logic, and innovation roadmap.
* Business impact when DevOps data becomes unavailable—lost productivity, compliance gaps, reputational risk, and delivery delays.
* Limitations of native recycle bins, scripts, and point tools (short retention, missing metadata, security gaps, scale issues).
* How Commvault delivers enterprise-grade DevOps protection with:
-- Single-pane-of-glass management
-- Unlimited retention & bundled storage
-- Automatic token rotation to avoid API rate limits
-- Cross-platform restore between GitHub, GitLab, and Azure DevOps
-- Granular recovery of repos, pipelines, artifacts, boards, and metadata
-- Auditability, RBAC, security-first design
* Announcement: Early Access Program for Jira protection beginning December.
* Live product demo: onboarding an app, configuring storage/retention, managing backups, and executing in-place or cross-cloud restores.

Chapters:
00:00 Introduction to DevOps Resilience
03:14 Understanding the Growing Risks in DevOps
05:54 The Importance of Resilience in DevOps
08:48 Identifying Risks and Their Impacts
12:11 Current Solutions and Their Limitations
15:09 Commvault's Approach to DevOps Resilience
17:53 Product Demonstration of Commvault Solutions
23:49 Conclusion and Key Takeaways

TL;DR
DevOps has become business-critical infrastructure—and native tools or scripts are no longer enough. Learn how Commvault provides scalable, secure, cross-platform protection for code, pipelines, boards, metadata, and more—helping teams recover fast from outages, human error, insider threats, or ransomware.

Key Takeaways
* DevOps is no longer just a developer workflow; it’s mission-critical infrastructure.
* Issues in code repositories or pipelines can cascade across the entire software delivery chain.
* Native tools and scripts cannot meet enterprise expectations for scale, retention, security, and compliance.
* Commvault simplifies protection with a unified, scalable, secure cloud-to-cloud recovery approach.
* Cross-platform restores ensure resilience even when major cloud providers experience outages.
* Treat DevOps data with the same rigor as any other business-critical data asset.

Who Is This Session For?
* CIO, CTO — Responsible for enterprise resilience and innovation velocity
* CISO, Security Leaders — Focused on protecting source code, pipelines & DevOps tooling
* DevOps & Platform Engineering Teams — Managing CI/CD, automation, and multi-cloud environments
* Cloud Architects & SREs — Ensuring availability, recoverability, and operational consistency
* IT Operations & Compliance Teams — Needing visibility, auditability, and scalable protection
* Product & Engineering Leaders — Safeguarding intellectual property and delivery pipelines

FAQ
Q: Why aren’t native DevOps recycle bins enough?
A: They offer short retention, inconsistent metadata capture, and no protection from platform-wide outages or insider threats. They’re not built for long-term, enterprise-grade recovery.

Q: Can Commvault handle thousands of repos without hitting API rate limits?
A: Yes. Automatic token rotation and intelligent workload distribution ensure backups run in parallel without breaking API quotas.

Q: What can I restore across platforms?
A: Repos, pipelines, boards, metadata, artifacts, and more—across GitHub, GitLab, Azure DevOps, and soon Jira.

Q: Does this help with compliance?
A: Yes. Centralized policies, RBAC, audit logs, and long-term retention simplify governance and regulatory alignment.

#DevOps #CyberResilience #Commvault #DevOpsSecurity #CloudBackup #GitHub #AzureDevOps

It’s the only cyber resilience platform built for the hybrid world, offering the best data security for all workloads, anywhere combined with the rapid, enterprise-scale recovery. In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, speed and agility are crucial for successful application development. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) offers a comprehensive environment for building, deploying, and managing cloud applications. To help developers and IT teams harness the full potential of SAP BTP, SAP provides extensive SAP BTP DevOps Training Videos. These tutorials are designed to guide you through DevOps best practices, automation, continuous integration, and continuous delivery (CI/CD) processes tailored for SAP BTP. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced professional, these videos can significantly boost your development efficiency.

Topics List:
Introduction to SAP BTP DevOps
Overview of DevOps Principles and Best Practices
Setting Up Your SAP BTP DevOps Environment
Continuous Integration with SAP BTP
Automating Builds and Deployments
Version Control with Git in SAP BTP
Configuring CI/CD Pipelines for SAP Applications
Using SAP Cloud SDK for DevOps Automation
Monitoring and Logging in SAP BTP DevOps
Security Best Practices in SAP DevOps
Managing Environments and Releases
Automating Testing and Quality Checks
Collaboration and Team Workflows
Integrating SAP BTP DevOps with External Tools
Advanced DevOps Strategies for SAP BTP
What Are SAP BTP DevOps Training Videos?
SAP BTP DevOps Training Videos are detailed visual tutorials that cover the entire lifecycle of application development, deployment, and management on SAP BTP. These videos explore how to implement DevOps practices effectively within SAP’s cloud environment, focusing on automation, continuous integration, and continuous delivery. They are suitable for developers, DevOps engineers, and IT administrators who want to streamline their workflows, improve deployment frequency, and ensure high-quality software releases.

Why Are These Training Videos Important?
Adopting DevOps is a game-changer for modern enterprises. SAP BTP DevOps Training Videos help teams understand how to automate their development pipelines, reduce manual errors, and accelerate release cycles. They also emphasize best practices for securing deployments, managing environments, and maintaining high availability. These tutorials are regularly updated, reflecting new SAP BTP features and industry standards, so your team remains current with the latest tools and techniques.

Key Topics Covered in SAP BTP DevOps Training Videos
1. Introduction to SAP BTP DevOps:
Learn about the core concepts of DevOps, why it’s essential for SAP cloud applications, and how SAP BTP supports DevOps workflows.

2. Setting Up Your DevOps Environment:
Configure your SAP BTP account, integrate with version control systems like Git, and prepare your development pipeline.

3. Building CI/CD Pipelines:
Create automated pipelines for code compilation, testing, and deployment using SAP tools and external platforms like Jenkins or GitHub Actions.

4. Automating Builds and Deployments:
Leverage SAP Cloud SDK and other automation tools to streamline application builds and deployment processes.

5. Monitoring and Logging:
Implement monitoring solutions to track application health, performance, and errors during and after deployment.

6. Securing DevOps Processes:
Apply security best practices, including secret management, role-based access, and secure pipelines.

7. Managing Multiple Environments:
Configure and manage development, testing, staging, and production environments efficiently.

8. Testing Automation:
Integrate automated testing at various pipeline stages to ensure code quality and functionality.

9. Collaboration and Team Workflows:
Utilize SAP BTP tools to foster collaboration among development, operations, and security teams.

10. Advanced DevOps Strategies:
Explore containerization, microservices, and hybrid cloud deployment strategies for SAP applications.

How to Maximize Your Learning with SAP BTP DevOps Videos
To make the most of these tutorials, practice setting up your own pipelines, experiment with automation tools, and integrate testing and monitoring into your workflows. Engage with SAP developer communities, participate in webinars, and stay updated on new features to continually refine your skills.

videoTitle: 20260325 Building a DevOps Pipeline with Cloud Build Trigger on Google Cloud Platform
videoDescription:
👉 This demo walks through how to build a DevOps pipeline on Google Cloud Platform using a Cloud Build trigger that automatically runs whenever code is pushed to a GitHub repository. When triggered, it deploys a simple Flask application to a VM instance, where users can access a “Hello Build Trigger” webpage over HTTP or HTTPS. The entire workflow is defined in the cloudbuild.yaml file.

Step 1. The pipeline begins by detecting the active project and determining the allowed region and zones, ensuring that all resources are created in the correct and permitted environment.

Step 2. It then builds a Docker image for the Flask application using the source code from the repository, preparing it for deployment.

Step 3. Next, the pipeline either creates a new VM instance or updates an existing one. It installs Docker, Nginx, and OpenSSL, runs the Docker container for the Flask app, generates a self-signed SSL certificate, and configures Nginx as a reverse proxy to handle HTTPS traffic. If the VM already exists, it skips the setup and simply redeploys the updated image.

Step 4. Finally, it sets up firewall rules to allow incoming HTTP and HTTPS traffic, making the application accessible from the internet.

✅ GCP DevOps Pipeline Demo – Written Guide
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1nIz91opc7jwgZ_yi3YOq8nS45-WG25lwb2WHZ2I_-zY
✅ GitHub repo
https://github.com/nov05/gcp-devops-repo-example/

videoTitle: Prometheus Explained in ONE Sentence | DevOps Simplified
videoDescription:
Understanding Prometheus doesn’t have to be complicated. In this short video, we explain Prometheus in one simple sentence, making it perfect for beginners, DevOps enthusiasts, and IT professionals looking to master monitoring tools quickly.

Prometheus is an open-source monitoring tool widely used in modern DevOps environments. It collects system metrics like CPU usage, memory, request latency, and error rates. Once metrics are collected, Prometheus can alert teams when thresholds are crossed or something goes wrong, ensuring potential outages or performance issues are detected before users notice them.

Monitoring is a critical part of DevOps, cloud engineering, and site reliability engineering (SRE). By proactively monitoring systems with Prometheus, organizations can improve uptime, maintain seamless user experiences, and reduce operational risks.

Whether you are managing corporate training platforms, retail applications, or cloud infrastructure, learning Prometheus is essential for staying ahead in today’s competitive tech environment.

💡 If you are learning DevOps, this short video is a perfect bite-sized explanation you can save, share, and revisit anytime.

✅ Follow this channel for more quick DevOps tutorials, Prometheus tips, and monitoring hacks to level up your IT skills!
Ref: https://prometheus.io/

videoTitle: 5 Skills Every College Student Must Learn (Cloud, AI & DevOps) 🚀
videoDescription:
College is no longer just about textbooks and exams. In today’s tech-driven world, students who learn Cloud, AI, DevOps, and core programming skills get a huge advantage in internships, placements, and real-world projects.

In this short video, you’ll discover five must-learn tools and skills that every college-going student should start learning as early as possible. These are not advanced or scary skills — they are beginner-friendly and widely used by professionals across software, data, and IT industries.

We cover GitHub for version control, Python or Java for programming fundamentals, Cloud platforms like AWS or Google Cloud, basic AI concepts and tools, and Operating System knowledge that every tech student must understand. Together, these skills build a strong foundation for careers in software development, DevOps, data science, and cloud engineering.

The goal of this video is to give you clarity — not overwhelm you. You don’t need to master everything at once. Start small, practice daily, and grow step by step. Even learning the basics of these tools during college can make your resume stand out and boost your confidence.

This is a quick 15–20 second YouTube Short, designed for busy students who want real value in less time. Save this video, share it with your friends, and comment which skill you are learning right now.

videoTitle: SysAdmin Weekly - 011 - How is Agentic AI Changing DevOps?
videoDescription:
This week on SysAdmin Weekly, Andy is joined by Luke Orellana, a fellow IT war buddy from the MSP trenches who's now a Senior Engineering Manager at Microsoft (yes, that Microsoft) working with AI Agents on a daily basis!

In this episode we unpack the good, the bad, and the "seriously, who thought YAML was a good idea"? parts of Infra-as-Code from Terraform and Pulumi to PowerShell DSC and Packer pipelines. Luke drops wisdom on platform engineering, the rise of AI agents with their impact on DevOps, and how he rewrote entire Terraform libraries because apparently, sleep is optional.

Also in this episode:

- Why password resets are a scam (Forrester says $70 a pop — no thanks).
- The glorious chaos of auditors asking for 30-day resets in 2025 (because security theater must go on).
- Andy’s obsession with Linux Mint Debian Edition and the Tux shrine on his desk.
- The legendary Domino’s Pizza Terraform provider. Yes. That’s real.
 
We also answer critical questions like:

- Can an AI agent wreck your entire Git repo?
- Should sysadmins fear change or just automate it?
- And what’s more powerful: Terraform CDK or the sheer willpower of a sysadmin trying to avoid YAML?

Grab your favorite caffeine source, commit to main (regrets optional), and get ready to laugh, learn, and question your life choices. This one’s got code, chaos, and caffeine-fueled commentary.

Episode Resources Below!

videoTitle: Master Cloud Computing Autoscaling: Save Money and Boost Performance! #devops #cloud #autoscaling
videoDescription:
Autoscaling is a game-changer in cloud computing, allowing you to dynamically adjust computational resources based on the load on your server farm. This means you can automatically increase or decrease the number of servers running behind your web application, optimizing for both performance and cost efficiency. 💡

In this video, I explore how autoscaling works, its benefits, and why it's essential for modern cloud infrastructure. Learn how to handle sudden traffic spikes without wasting resources, ensuring your applications run smoothly and cost-effectively. Don't miss out on mastering this vital cloud-computing technique!

videoTitle: InFocus - Honeycomb: Accept Code is Never Going to Be Perfect #LaunchDarkly #DevOps #FeatureFlags
videoDescription:
I used to be on call 24/7 as a team lead, no vacations, no unplugging. All because of one big lie: that software could be perfect before it hits production.

Reality? It never is.

What actually matters isn’t perfect code. It’s having control. The ability to roll back instantly, fix fast, and adapt when things go wrong.

That’s the shift: from chasing perfection to building confidence in change.
channelDescription:
LaunchDarkly isn’t just a leader in feature management — it’s the first scalable feature management platform. Feature management allows development teams to innovate faster by fundamentally transforming how software is delivered to customers. With the ability to gradually release new software features to any segment of users on any platform, DevOps teams can standardize safe releases at scale, accelerate their journey to the cloud and collaborate more effectively with business teams. 

Today, LaunchDarkly deploys peaks of 20 trillion feature flags each day, and that number continues to grow. Founded in 2014 in Oakland, California by Edith Harbaugh and John Kodumal, LaunchDarkly has been named on the Forbes Cloud 100 list, InfoWorld’s 2021 Technology of the Year list, and the Enterprise Tech 30 list. Learn more at launchdarkly.com.

https://launchdarkly.com/

videoTitle: Ways to make money (#4): Cloud and DevOps  #techcareers #careeradvice
videoDescription:
Cloud and DevOps engineers keep production alive when outages, bad deploys, and latency spikes hit. From Linux and Docker to Kubernetes, Terraform, AWS, Azure, CI/CD, dashboards, alerts, and SRE workflows, this is one of the highest-pressure paths in tech.

If you want a high-paying tech career, cloud engineering, DevOps, and site reliability engineering offer strong salaries, real demand, and a fast path in for builders who can automate and stay calm in chaos. The trade-off: on-call stress, constant change, and invisible work when systems run smoothly.

Follow for more high-income tech career breakdowns, software engineering salary facts, and real-world job paths.  
#CloudEngineering #DevOps #SRE #TechCareers #AWS #Kubernetes #Linux
